_note: Aura (trust-theater surface) — the trust sub-council, one defect per critic lens (Ann C. / Cat W. / Kevin W.) + ST5.
_provenance: RE-SOURCED from real council transcripts (I-13 deeper fix): each phrasing below is a wording the trust sub-council ACTUALLY produced in runs/2026-06-12-aura-trust-baseline.md / 2026-06-12-aura-trust-run2.md / 2026-06-12-aura-trust-run3.md — lifted from the per-defect Caught-by quotes, lightly trimmed of bold/byline scaffolding, not author-invented.

T1 privacy: opt-out default / speculativ: the do-nothing default is maximal collection; opting out is a per-source chore
a surveillance architecture with a privacy policy stapled to the footer; collect and retain everything indefinitely
Privacy and growth are a trade-off named as the false dichotomy; growth over privacy
Accept all bundled consent — coerced bundling, not freely given
deletion that doesn't delete — Delete my account disables sign-in, the data is retained
T2 control: auto-acts, no visibility int: no way for the user to see what Aura inferred, and no way to correct or override — a one-way mirror
it demos really well is precisely the thing I refuse to accept as evidence — no eval behind auto-archive, auto-reply, auto-accept
the assistant auto-archives, auto-replies, and auto-accepts with no eval behind the classifier
the assistant auto-acts with no way to correct or override and no undo on irreversible auto-send
load-bearing capability claims with no evals — the whole product rides on a vibe
T3 deployment: safety validated once, no: Safety frozen at launch — no production monitoring, no re-check on model swap
the safety posture explicitly assumes the model never changes; no monitoring, no re-checking safety when we upgrade
we treat model swaps as a backend detail — refused
Harm is a policy problem, not a design problem — pushed onto the Terms of Service
Safety frozen at the launch snapshot; no re-check on model swap, no plan for re-checking safety when we upgrade
